thought.text:
Beautiful — LICM hoisted the loads and several muls. The loop body has just `mul, mul, mul, add chain, sdiv, srem` which is much simpler. Total useful work per iteration: ~10 instructions vs 10000.

Now let me also recognize: `%5 = mul %4, 3`, `%6 = mul %5, 3` — these are loop-invariant too (since %4 is invariant)! Why didn't LICM catch them? Because they were created by the SimplifyBinop / reassoc transformations AFTER LICM ran.

I need to run LICM again after the rest of simplification. Already doing that in the code. But the rerun may not be detecting because `%5 = mul %4, 3` has operands `%4` (defined in preheader, so loop-invariant) and `3` (constant, invariant). Both invariant. So should be hoisted by LICM.

Wait, my code does LICM at start AND end. The end LICM should hoist these. Let me trace — maybe the issue is something else.

Looking again, only 4 muls hoisted out, but 3 more muls remain in body. So LICM rerun only hoisted some. Let me investigate. Actually after LICM, simplify might run again and create new opportunities, and I should re-run LICM iteratively.

Let me make the whole thing iterate to fixpoint:

tool_call.name: file_editor
